intro: Mariscus bidwellii, also known as Bidwell's Sedge, is a type of plant belonging to the Cyperaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Mariscus bidwellii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
